briefs.title: Burnaby updates its bylaw notice enforcement rules — fines and notices may change
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed Amendment No. 2 to its Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw, updating the rules around how the city issues and enforces bylaw violation notices (official written warnings or fines for breaking local rules). Businesses operating in Burnaby should check whether any fines, notice procedures, or dispute steps that apply to them have changed under the updated bylaw.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw No. 14807 to check if your business is affected.

briefs.title: Burnaby raises municipal fees and charges under updated Bylaw No. 14806
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed Bylaw No. 14806, the second amendment to its Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw for 2026. This update changes the fees the City of Burnaby charges for various permits, licences, and municipal services — meaning some costs for doing business or developing property in Burnaby may now be higher.
briefs.action_required: Check Burnaby's updated fee schedule to see if any permits or licences your business uses have changed in cost.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Burnaby updates parking meter and EV charging meter rules with new bylaw amendment
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed Bylaw No. 14805, which amends the rules governing parking meters and electric vehicle (EV) charging meters in the city. This update changes how parking and EV charging is regulated in Burnaby, though the specific fee or operational changes were not detailed in the announcement.
briefs.action_required: Check Burnaby's updated bylaw for any new parking or EV charging rules that affect your business location.
briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Zoning change proposed for 2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue — first two readings passed
briefs.summary: City council passed the first and second readings of a zoning bylaw amendment (a change to the rules about how land at a specific address can be used or developed) for the properties at 2209 and 2225 Springer Avenue. The amendment must still complete further readings and approval before it takes effect.
briefs.action_required: If you own or lease property near Springer Avenue, monitor upcoming council readings for final approval.

briefs.title: Rezoning application at 6800 Lougheed Highway advances to next stage of approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council gave first and second reading to a zoning bylaw amendment (a formal step that changes what a property can legally be used or built on) for the site at 6800 Lougheed Highway. This moves the rezoning application REZ #25-18 forward in the approval process, with further readings and a public hearing still required before any change is finalized.
briefs.action_required: If your business or property is near 6800 Lougheed Hwy, monitor the City of Burnaby website for the upcoming public hearing date.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Zoning change at 5058 Imperial Street moves forward despite council opposition
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council gave first and second reading to a zoning bylaw amendment (a change to the rules about how a specific property can be used or developed) for 5058 Imperial Street. The vote passed but faced pushback from four council members.
briefs.action_required: If your business or property is near 5058 Imperial Street, monitor future council readings and hearings for this zoning change.

briefs.title: Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan Implementation Action Plan project formally initiated
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has received and acknowledged the project initiation for the Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan (OCP) Implementation Action Plan. An OCP is a long-term planning document that guides how land is used, developed, and built on across the city — this action plan sets out the steps to put that vision into practice.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: City releases 2025 investment results and 2026 forecast — no action required for businesses
briefs.summary: City council received a report summarizing how the city's investment program performed in 2025 and what returns are expected in 2026. This is an internal financial update for the city — it does not create new rules, fees, or requirements for local businesses.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council approves Poverty Reduction Strategy and seeks provincial funding renewal
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially approved a Poverty Reduction Strategy and directed staff to build an action plan alongside community partners. The Mayor has also been asked to push the provincial government to renew funding through UBCM (the Union of BC Municipalities, a body that channels provincial money to local governments) for poverty reduction work.

briefs.title: Five-Storey Self-Storage Facility at 2757 Norland Avenue Rezoning Approved to Advance
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has directed staff to prepare a rezoning bylaw (an official rule change that allows a new type of building on a specific property) to permit a five-storey self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue. No public hearing is required because the proposal aligns with the Official Community Plan (the city's long-term land use guide).
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: 12-Storey Mixed-Use Building with 290 Units Approved for 6535 Kingsway, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has directed staff to prepare a rezoning bylaw (a formal rule change that allows a different type of building on a property) for 6535 Kingsway. The approved plan allows a 12-storey building with 290 residential units and ground-floor commercial space.
briefs.action_required: If you operate near 6535 Kingsway, monitor the rezoning bylaw process for public hearing dates.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Mixed-use towers at 3900 Grand Promenade to swap office space for hotel, restaurant, and 362 new strata units
briefs.summary: Council has approved moving forward with a bylaw to change the approved development at 3900 Grand Promenade. The plan now replaces office space with hotel and restaurant uses, adds 362 strata units (privately owned condos), and moves the affordable housing requirement to a nearby site called Carrigan.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the rezoning bylaw process if your business operates near or plans to invest in 3900 Grand Promenade.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service

briefs.title: Carrigan Court mixed-use development rezoning application accepted by council
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved staff to accept a rezoning application (a formal request to change how land can be used) for 3856 and 3870 Carrigan Court. The proposed development would bring high-density housing and commercial space to the site, combining strata units (individually owned condos) and rental units.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Real Estate

briefs.title: $1.155M asphalt paving contract renewed with Blackrete Paving by municipal council
briefs.summary: A local municipal council has renewed its asphalt paving services contract with Blackrete Paving Ltd. for an estimated $1,155,000 including taxes. This is a government contract renewal for road and paving work under the Engineering Department — no new rules or regulations are introduced.

briefs.title: 19 contractors awarded hired equipment services contracts paid by actual quantities delivered
briefs.summary: A local council has approved contracts with 19 separate contractors to provide hired equipment services. Payments to these contractors will be based on the actual amount of work or materials delivered, not a fixed price agreed in advance.

briefs.title: Modular Four-Plex Housing Pilot Contract Sent Back for Efficiency Review
briefs.summary: City council has sent the contract award for a modular four-plex (a prefabricated building with four housing units) pilot project at 8315 14th Avenue back to staff. The decision was made to allow staff to review the project more closely and find ways to manage it more efficiently before moving forward.

briefs.title: $4.2M water metering contract awarded to Neptune Technology Group Canada
briefs.summary: A municipal council has awarded a $4.2 million contract to Neptune Technology Group Canada Co. to supply and deliver water metering technology. This is a public procurement (government purchasing) decision that signals upcoming infrastructure upgrades to the local water system.

briefs.title: BC municipality approves $36,250.57 in UBCM membership dues for 2026
briefs.summary: A BC municipal council has approved paying $36,250.57 (including taxes) in membership dues to the Union of BC Municipalities (UBCM) for 2026. UBCM is the association that represents BC's local governments — this payment covers the municipality's annual membership fee.

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority approves 2026–2030 Strategic Plan, shaping city housing development
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved the Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) Corporation's five-year strategic plan for 2026–2030. This plan will guide how the BHA develops and manages affordable housing in Burnaby over the next five years, which may affect local construction, development, and property management activity.
